stemHtml: Which of the following statements correctly describes a cold-air drop and one of the potential hazards associated with it?

options.a: A low pressure system moves over a cold surface causing icing that extends into at least the mid-levels.
options.b: Cold air becomes trapped, creating an area of low pressure aloft, which causes instability.
options.c: Cold air is advected from a cold high coastal plain dropping down onto a warm sea surface causing well-developed convection clouds and severe icing.
options.d: Warm air is advected by the upper airflow, which causes a cold low-level jetstream to form just above the inversion layer.
